revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) |Group={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |LatitudeDeg=34|LatitudeMin=39|LatitudeSec=49.69 |LongitudeDeg=133|LongitudeMin=58|LongitudeSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Size=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |Map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |Label=Kanakurayama Kofun |LabelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) |Group={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |LatitudeDeg=34|LatitudeMin=39|LatitudeSec=49.69 |LongitudeDeg=133|LongitudeMin=58|LongitudeSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Size=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |Map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |Label=Kanakurayama Kofun |LabelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) |Group={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |LatitudeDeg=34|LatitudeMin=39|LatitudeSec=49.69 |LongitudeDeg=133|LongitudeMin=58|LongitudeSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |Size=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) |Group={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |LatitudeDeg=34|LatitudeMin=39|LatitudeSec=49.69 |LongitudeDeg=133|LongitudeMin=58|LongitudeSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) |Group={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |LatitudeDeg=34|LatitudeMin=39|LatitudeSec=49.69 |LongitudeDeg=133|LongitudeMin=58|LongitudeSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. 18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. 18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinatesDeg=34|coordinatesMin=39|coordinatesSec=49.69 |coordinatesDeg=133|coordinatesMin=58|coordinatesSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinatesDeg=34|coordinatesMin=39|coordinatesSec=49.69 |coordinatesDeg=133|coordinatesMin=58|coordinatesSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |Burial Facility=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinatesDeg=34|coordinatesMin=39|coordinatesSec=49.69 |coordinatesDeg=133|coordinatesMin=58|coordinatesSec=3.95 |ISO=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. 18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |Icon=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_labelPosition=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant artifacts during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous artifacts.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The artifacts include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == Artifacts == Significant artifacts include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits artifacts from Kanakurayama Kofun. Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == Burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun.
