But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]

The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. 18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]

18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金蔵山古墳 up to rev 101786603
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 金蔵山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[操山古墳群]] |所在地 =[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]沢田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 49.69 |経度度 = 133|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 3.95 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長165m
高さ18m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基（内部に[[割竹形木棺]]） |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]]片・[[土師器]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半-[[5世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 岡山県第4位の規模 |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 |ラベル = 金蔵山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Misaoyama, enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[操山]]（北方より）}}{{small|左2番目の山頂に金蔵山古墳が所在する。手前は[[百間川]]。}}]] '''金蔵山古墳'''（かなくらやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]沢田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[操山古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 岡山県では第4位の規模の古墳で岡山県における主な古墳は次の通り。 # [[造山古墳]]（岡山市北区新庄下） - 墳丘長350メートル。 # [[作山古墳]]（総社市三須） - 墳丘長282メートル。 # [[両宮山古墳]]（赤磐市穂崎） - 墳丘長206メートル。 # '''金蔵山古墳'''（岡山市中区沢田） - 墳丘長165メートル。 、[[4世紀]]後半-[[5世紀]]初頭（[[古墳時代]]前期-中期）頃の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地中心部から東方の独立山塊である[[操山]]山塊において、ほぼ中央の金蔵山（標高100メートル）山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に石室が調査され副葬品が出土しているほか、[[2000年]]度（[[平成]]12年度）以降に墳丘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北方向に向ける{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成。墳丘長は165メートルを測り、岡山県では第4位の規模になる。墳丘外表では各段の[[円筒埴輪]]列のほか、[[形象埴輪]]、[[葺石]]が認められる。また墳丘くびれ部には、西側に[[造出]]、東側に島状遺構を付す{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。埋葬施設は2基の[[竪穴式石室]]（中央石室・南石室）で、いずれも埴輪方形区画中に構築され、特に中央石室は副葬品埋納用の副室を伴う{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。中央石室主室・南石室は盗掘に遭っているが{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、調査において多くの副葬品が出土している。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期-中期の[[4世紀]]後半-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。[[造山古墳]]が築造されるまでは中国・四国・九州地方で最大規模であり{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}、墳丘規模・豊富な副葬品の点で吉備地方の代表的古墳の1つであるとともに{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}、内容が明らかとなった大型古墳である点でも重要視される古墳になる[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html 金蔵山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）、古墳の一部の発掘。以後も発掘され多くの出土品が出土{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、発掘調査（倉敷考古館、[[1959年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 * [[2000年]]度（[[平成]]12年度）、後円部墳端の調査（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、[[2008年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}。 * [[2014年]]度（平成26年度）以降、範囲確認調査（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、[[2019年|2019]]・[[2024年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|第1次調査 現地説明会資料|2015}}{{Sfn|第2次調査 現地説明会資料|2016}}{{Sfn|第3次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：165メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：110メートル ** 高さ：18メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：72メートル ** 高さ：15.5メートル 墳丘の西くびれ部には[[造出]]、東くびれ部には島状遺構を伴う{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。これらの発掘調査では、円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪とともに祭祀空間を表現した家形・柵形・囲形埴輪などが検出されている{{Sfn|第1次調査 現地説明会資料|2015}}{{Sfn|第2次調査 現地説明会資料|2016}}{{Sfn|第3次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|東島状遺構
{{small|左に墳丘と接続する土橋。2018年調査時。}} Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-2.jpg|東島状遺構
{{small|左に島状遺構、右に墳丘、中央上に土橋。右下の柵状埴輪をはじめ多くの埴輪が出土した。2018年調査時。}} Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|西造出 金蔵山古墳 建物形埴輪3 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|建物形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 囲形埴輪1 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|囲形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 建物形埴輪1 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|建物形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 囲形埴輪2 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|囲形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 建物形埴輪2 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|建物形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 柵形埴輪 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|柵形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪1 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪2 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|手前に南石室開口部。右上に中央石室主室が所在。}}]] 埋葬施設としては、後円部墳頂において[[竪穴式石室]]2基の構築が認められている{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。1基は後円部中央に（中央石室）、もう1基はその南側に位置し（南石室）、特に中央石室は副葬品埋納用の副室を伴う{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。中央石室・南石室はいずれも墳丘主軸に直交する向きに構築され、周囲には埴輪方形区画が設けられる{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。区画の埴輪には、円筒埴輪のほか、形象埴輪として盾形・靫形・短甲形・草摺形・蓋形・高坏形・家形・鶏形・水鳥形埴輪がある{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。各石室の詳細は次の通り。 :; 中央石室（主室） :: 後円部中央、東西10メートル・南北7メートルの墓壙に構築され、墓壙底には排水のための砂利を敷く{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室は石英粗面岩の板石の小口積みにより、長さ6.1メートル・東端幅1.3メートル・西端幅1.15メートル・深さ0.80メートルを測り、壁面にはベンガラが塗布される{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室内では粘土床に[[割竹形木棺]]が据えられ、粘土床の下部には排水溝を設ける{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この石室は盗掘に遭っているが{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、調査において少数の副葬品が出土している（[[#出土品|後述]]：以下同様）{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 :; 中央石室副室 :: 主室の東側において、主室に連続する形で設けられる{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。南北1.6メートル・東西0.75メートル・深さ0.80メートルを測る{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この副室は調査時点で完存しており{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、石室内には埴輪盒4個が置かれ、盒内を大半として盒内外から多くの副葬品が出土している{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 ;; 南石室 :: 中央石室の南側、東西約12メートル・南北3.9メートルの墓壙に構築される{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室は石英粗面岩の割石の小口積みにより、長さ7.2メートル・東端幅1.35メートル・深さ約0.85メートルを測り、[[花崗岩]]の天井石7枚で覆われる{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この石室は盗掘に遭っているが{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、調査において変形二神四獣鏡をはじめとする多くの副葬品が出土している。 なお前方部でも形象埴輪が検出されているため、前方部にも埋葬施設が存在する可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 File:Kanakurayama Kofun, minami-sekishitsu.jpg|南石室内部 == 出土品 == 古墳からの主な出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" ! 中央石室主室!!中央石室副室!!南石室!!石室外 |- |style="vertical-align:top"|碧玉製管玉 15

{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 関連施設 == * 倉敷考古館（倉敷市中央） - 金蔵山古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2008|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4402 |title=金蔵山古墳 -後円部墳端の調査-|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|金蔵山古墳|2008}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000214930.pdf 範囲確認調査現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第1次調査 現地説明会資料|2015}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2015年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000249082.pdf 範囲確認調査（第2次）現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第2次調査 現地説明会資料|2016}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2016年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000305699.pdf 範囲確認調査（第3次）現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第3次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2017年）。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf 範囲確認調査（第4次）現地説明会資料]}}|ref={{Harvid|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}}}（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、2018年）。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=鎌木義昌|chapter=金蔵山古墳|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|金蔵山古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=間壁忠彦|year=|chapter=金蔵山古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=金蔵山古墳|title=岡山県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]34|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|金蔵山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=金蔵山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=西谷真治・鎌木義昌|year=1959|chapter=|title=金蔵山古墳|series=倉敷考古館研究報告第1冊|publisher=倉敷考古館|isbn=|ref=}} ** 複製：{{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=西谷真治・鎌木義昌|year=1989|chapter=|title=金蔵山古墳|series=倉敷考古館研究報告第1冊|publisher=木耳社|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2019|chapter=|title=金蔵山古墳 -範囲確認発掘調査報告-|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=金蔵山古墳 -範囲確認発掘調査報告II-|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[操山古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kanakurayama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html 金蔵山古墳] - 岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」 {{DEFAULTSORT:かなくらやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[金蔵山古墳]] to [[Kanakurayama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 金蔵山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左手前に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[操山古墳群]] |所在地 =[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]沢田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 49.69 |経度度 = 133|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 3.95 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長165m
高さ18m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基（内部に[[割竹形木棺]]） |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]]片・[[土師器]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半-[[5世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 岡山県第4位の規模 |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北西 |ラベル = 金蔵山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Misaoyama, enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[操山]]（北方より）}}{{small|左2番目の山頂に金蔵山古墳が所在する。手前は[[百間川]]。}}]] '''金蔵山古墳'''（かなくらやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[中区 (岡山市)|中区]]沢田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[操山古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 岡山県では第4位の規模の古墳で岡山県における主な古墳は次の通り。 # [[造山古墳]]（岡山市北区新庄下） - 墳丘長350メートル。 # [[作山古墳]]（総社市三須） - 墳丘長282メートル。 # [[両宮山古墳]]（赤磐市穂崎） - 墳丘長206メートル。 # '''金蔵山古墳'''（岡山市中区沢田） - 墳丘長165メートル。 、[[4世紀]]後半-[[5世紀]]初頭（[[古墳時代]]前期-中期）頃の築造と推定される。 == 概要 == 岡山県南部、岡山市街地中心部から東方の独立山塊である[[操山]]山塊において、ほぼ中央の金蔵山（標高100メートル）山頂に築造された大型前方後円墳である{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に石室が調査され副葬品が出土しているほか、[[2000年]]度（[[平成]]12年度）以降に墳丘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北方向に向ける{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘は後円部で3段築成、前方部で2段築成。墳丘長は165メートルを測り、岡山県では第4位の規模になる。墳丘外表では各段の[[円筒埴輪]]列のほか、[[形象埴輪]]、[[葺石]]が認められる。また墳丘くびれ部には、西側に[[造出]]、東側に島状遺構を付す{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。埋葬施設は2基の[[竪穴式石室]]（中央石室・南石室）で、いずれも埴輪方形区画中に構築され、特に中央石室は副葬品埋納用の副室を伴う{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。中央石室主室・南石室は盗掘に遭っているが{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、調査において多くの副葬品が出土している。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期-中期の[[4世紀]]後半-[[5世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。[[造山古墳]]が築造されるまでは中国・四国・九州地方で最大規模であり{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}、墳丘規模・豊富な副葬品の点で吉備地方の代表的古墳の1つであるとともに{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}、内容が明らかとなった大型古墳である点でも重要視される古墳になる[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html 金蔵山古墳]（岡山市シティミュージアム「[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/bunkazai_top/index.html 岡山市の文化財探訪]」）。。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1884年]]（[[明治]]17年）、古墳の一部の発掘。以後も発掘され多くの出土品が出土{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、発掘調査（倉敷考古館、[[1959年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 * [[2000年]]度（[[平成]]12年度）、後円部墳端の調査（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、[[2008年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳|2008}}。 * [[2014年]]度（平成26年度）以降、範囲確認調査（岡山市埋蔵文化財センター、[[2019年|2019]]・[[2024年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|第1次調査 現地説明会資料|2015}}{{Sfn|第2次調査 現地説明会資料|2016}}{{Sfn|第3次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * 墳丘長：165メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：110メートル ** 高さ：18メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 幅：72メートル ** 高さ：15.5メートル 墳丘の西くびれ部には[[造出]]、東くびれ部には島状遺構を伴う{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。これらの発掘調査では、円筒埴輪・朝顔形埴輪とともに祭祀空間を表現した家形・柵形・囲形埴輪などが検出されている{{Sfn|第1次調査 現地説明会資料|2015}}{{Sfn|第2次調査 現地説明会資料|2016}}{{Sfn|第3次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}{{Sfn|第4次調査 現地説明会資料|2018}}。 Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|東島状遺構
{{small|左に墳丘と接続する土橋。2018年調査時。}} Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-2.jpg|東島状遺構
{{small|左に島状遺構、右に墳丘、中央上に土橋。右下の柵状埴輪をはじめ多くの埴輪が出土した。2018年調査時。}} Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|西造出 金蔵山古墳 建物形埴輪3 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|建物形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 囲形埴輪1 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|囲形埴輪
{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} 金蔵山古墳 建物形埴輪1 (岡山シティミュージアム展示).JPG|建物形埴輪

{{small|岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}{{small|手前に南石室開口部。右上に中央石室主室が所在。}}]] 埋葬施設としては、後円部墳頂において[[竪穴式石室]]2基の構築が認められている{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。1基は後円部中央に（中央石室）、もう1基はその南側に位置し（南石室）、特に中央石室は副葬品埋納用の副室を伴う{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。中央石室・南石室はいずれも墳丘主軸に直交する向きに構築され、周囲には埴輪方形区画が設けられる{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。区画の埴輪には、円筒埴輪のほか、形象埴輪として盾形・靫形・短甲形・草摺形・蓋形・高坏形・家形・鶏形・水鳥形埴輪がある{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。各石室の詳細は次の通り。 :; 中央石室（主室） :: 後円部中央、東西10メートル・南北7メートルの墓壙に構築され、墓壙底には排水のための砂利を敷く{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室は石英粗面岩の板石の小口積みにより、長さ6.1メートル・東端幅1.3メートル・西端幅1.15メートル・深さ0.80メートルを測り、壁面にはベンガラが塗布される{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室内では粘土床に[[割竹形木棺]]が据えられ、粘土床の下部には排水溝を設ける{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この石室は盗掘に遭っているが{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、調査において少数の副葬品が出土している（[[#出土品|後述]]：以下同様）{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 :; 中央石室副室 :: 主室の東側において、主室に連続する形で設けられる{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。南北1.6メートル・東西0.75メートル・深さ0.80メートルを測る{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この副室は調査時点で完存しており{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、石室内には埴輪盒4個が置かれ、盒内を大半として盒内外から多くの副葬品が出土している{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 ;; 南石室 :: 中央石室の南側、東西約12メートル・南北3.9メートルの墓壙に構築される{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。石室は石英粗面岩の割石の小口積みにより、長さ7.2メートル・東端幅1.35メートル・深さ約0.85メートルを測り、[[花崗岩]]の天井石7枚で覆われる{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。この石室は盗掘に遭っているが{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（日本大百科全書）}}、調査において変形二神四獣鏡をはじめとする多くの副葬品が出土している。 なお前方部でも形象埴輪が検出されているため、前方部にも埋葬施設が存在する可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 File:Kanakurayama Kofun, minami-sekishitsu.jpg|南石室内部 == 出土品 == 古墳からの主な出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|金蔵山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" ! 中央石室主室!!中央石室副室!!南石室!!石室外 |- |style="vertical-align:top"|碧玉製管玉 15

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|12=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|12=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|12=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|12=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|12=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hajiki|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hajiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # {{ill|Zōzan Kofun|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōzan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|qq=}} (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # {{ill|Sakuyama Kofun|ja|作山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun|kk= (Sōja)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|qq=}} (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # {{ill|Ryōgūzan Kofun|lt=Ryōgūzan Kofun|lt=Ryōgūyama Kofun|ja|両宮山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōgūyama Kofun|qq=}} (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]]
