{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[en:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kanakurayama Kofun
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:岡山市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates=34|coordinates=39|coordinates=49.69 |coordinates=133|coordinates=58|coordinates=3.95 |omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Kanakurayama Kofun |image=[[File:Kanakurayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates={{Coord|34.663803|N|133.967764|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Kanakurayama Kofun]] [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[sn:anakurayama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}}

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates={{Coord|34.663803|N|133.967764|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55523295}}

The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View of the rear section from the front section. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, shima-1.jpg|Eastern island-like feature. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55523295}}

Mound (foreground left: front section, background right: rear section) | part_of={{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], Sawada |coordinates={{Coord|34.663803|N|133.967764|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted=JP-33 |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun |area=165 m mound length
18 m height (rear section) |burial=Two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (including split bamboo-shaped wooden coffins) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Date=Late 4th–early 5th century |Notable=None |map=Japan Okayama |type=Keyhole_kofun_NNW |map_dot_label=Kanakurayama Kofun |map_dot_label=top |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (金蔵山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Naka Ward (Okayama)|Naka Ward]], [[:en:Okayama City|Okayama City]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. This keyhole-shaped kofun is part of the {{ill|Misaoyama Kofun Group|ja|操山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misaoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} and is the fourth largest kofun in the prefecture.Major kofun in Okayama Prefecture: # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Okayama City, Kita Ward): 350 m mound length. # [[:en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Sōja)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] (Sōja City, Misuno): 282 m mound length. # [[:en:Ryōgūzan Kofun|Ryōgūyama Kofun]] (Akaiwa City, Hosaki): 206 m mound length. # '''Kanakurayama Kofun''' (Okayama City, Naka Ward, Sawada): 165 m mound length. == Overview == Kanakurayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun built atop the 100-meter-high summit of Kanakurayama, located centrally within the independent [[Sōzan]] mountain range in southern Okayama Prefecture. The mound, which faces north, was constructed during the late 4th to early 5th century (early to middle[[Kofun period]]).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|publisher=Okayama City Museum}} The mound features three-tier construction for the rear section and two tiers for the front section. The mound length is 165 m, making it the fourth largest in Okayama Prefecture. The outer surface of the mound exhibits {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, and stone paving. Unique elements include a west-side protrusion and an island-shaped feature on the east-side constriction.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okayama.jp/contents/000324468.pdf|title=Fourth Survey Results|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} The mound contains two {{ill|tateana-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (central and southern), both within rectangular haniwa enclosures. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods, and both chambers have been looted but yielded significant burial goods during excavation.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} == Excavation History == * 1884: Partial excavation revealed numerous burial goods.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun|author=Kurashiki Archaeological Museum}} * 1953: Excavation by Kurashiki Archaeological Museum, with findings published in 1959.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} * 2000: Investigation of the rear section.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Rear Section Survey-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * 2014–2018: Range confirmation surveys revealed new insights into the mound structure.{{Cite book|title=Fourth Survey Results}} == burial Chambers == Two stone chambers were identified at the mound's rear. The central chamber includes a sub-room for burial goods. The burial goods include weapons, tools, and decorative items such as mirrors and beads.{{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods include: * '''Central Chamber''': Jade beads, lacquerware fragments, and cylindrical copper implements. * '''Southern Chamber''': Two Gods-Four Beasts mirror, amber beads, and iron weapons. * '''Outside the chambers''': Eight-lobed mirrors and pottery, including Hajiki. File:Kanakurayama Kofun, tsukuridashi.jpg|Western projection. == Related Sites == * '''Kurashiki Archaeological Museum''': Exhibits burial goods from Kanakurayama Kofun. * [[Minato Chausuyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|ja|網浜茶臼山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ami Hama Chausuyama Kofun|qq=}} * (92 met ... 5 KB (707 words) - 01:04, == References == === notes === === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Kanakurayama Kofun -Mound Excavation Report-|publisher=Okayama City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-dō Shuppan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/02.html Kanakurayama Kofun - Okayama City Museum] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|金蔵山古墳|version=101786603|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:金蔵山古墳]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kanakurayama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55523295}}
